rulings.Case Title: 2856-62 WASHINGTON STREET HOME OWNERS VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEPARTMENT OF
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, July 22, 2016 line 1. DEFENDANTS IN INTERVENTION 2856-62 WASHINGTON STREET HOME OWNERS' ASSOCIATION, DANIEL MULDERRY, GAYLEN MULDERRY, PETER DUBOIS, LESLIE ZIMMERMAN, HENRIETTA HAGOPIAN DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IN INTERVENTION SUSTAINED with leave to amend to allege compliance with of exemption from Davis Sterling Act. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION VS. POPE & POPE PROPERTIES LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And/Or Adjudication As To (1) Wells Fargo Banks Complaint And (2) Defendants First Amended Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, July 22, 2016 line 2. PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And/Or Adjudication As To (1) Wells Fargo Banks Complaint And (2) Defendants First Amended Cross-Complaint GRANTED. Moving party shifted its burden and opposing party failed to show that there is a triable issue of fact or that Plaintiff is not entitled to a judgment as a matter of law. Guarantees are independent contract enforceable on their own terms. By the terms of the Deed of Trust, the guarantee was excluded from the obligations secured by the Deed of Trust. Plaintiff's non-judicial foreclosure and action for deficiency against the guarantor do not violate the one action rule. Additionally, guarantor executed waivers as contemplated by CC 2856. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JORGE SAUCEDO VS. JOSHUA RODDY ET AL (**case reclassified as regular civil**)
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike First And Second Notices To Quit From First Amended Complaint; Mpa
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, July 22, 2016 line 6. DEFENDANT JOSHUA RODDY's Motion To Strike First And Second Notices To Quit From First Amended Complaint DENIED. Notices are not irrelevant or improper. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JORGE SAUCEDO VS. JOSHUA RODDY ET AL (**case reclassified as regular civil**)
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants' Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To First Amended Complaint; Mpa
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, July 22, 2016 line 6. DEFENDANT JOSHUA RODDY's Demurrer To First Amended Complaint sustained in part and overruled in part. The demurrer is sustained as to Plaintiff Saucedo, despite being provided with an opportunity to amend, Saucedo failed to allege any facts establishing his standing to sue. Demurrer is otherwise overruled. Plaintiff sufficiently plead that "at least one of the grounds enumerated under Section 37.9(a) or (b) and also stated in the notice to vacate is the dominant motive for recovering possession." Defendant fails to state any authority for the proposition that "the dominant motive" allegation requires multiple motives to be asserted as "the dominant motive" or that the landlord cannot recover possession under multiple grounds enumerated under Section 37.9(a) or (b) asserting that only one of them is "the dominant motive." = (501/REQ)
